summary: Baxtori collects recent repository changes, publishes selected stories with code excerpts, and stores reader state.

reviews.summary: Repository scope, edition decisions, watches, questions, and history were reviewed together.
Origin-backed collection now follows one account's configured repository choices, exact re-review requests, and diff-addressed evidence while keeping reader state in a separate feedback system.
The Repo Map became an active learning loop: changed evidence reopens review, walkthroughs teach execution paths, and questions preserve uncertainty.

throughCommit.subject: Make the Baxtori map evolve and teach (#6)

questions.question: Should unattended reviews eventually use a GitHub App installation token instead of depending on the local checkout?
questions.whyItMatters: The browser session is intentionally interactive, while the scheduled review currently gets repository access from the local machine.

questions.question: Which map changes are safe to make deterministically, and which should always require review judgment?
questions.whyItMatters: Freshness can be derived from Git history, but concepts, decisions, and confidence should not be rewritten from filenames alone.

questions.question: When can the legacy Sites build path be removed without losing a working fallback?
questions.whyItMatters: Keeping two build paths is useful during migration but becomes maintenance debt after the Vercel deployment is proven.

questions.question: When should personal understanding move from device-local state to a private account-backed history?
questions.whyItMatters: Resolved by persisting versioned reader state behind the signed-in GitHub identity while retaining local fallback behavior when account sync is unavailable.

questions.question: When should compiler input use a stable indexed account identifier instead of scanning reader states by GitHub login?
questions.whyItMatters: The exported account boundary is explicit, but login-based collection is a personal-scale lookup and GitHub logins can change.

questions.question: Should repository inventory export degrade independently when its Convex function has not reached the deployed backend?
questions.whyItMatters: The collector has a deliberate configured-cache fallback, but the current Promise.all exporter prevents reaching it when only inventory is unavailable.

questions.question: How should Continue priorities and minute estimates be calibrated without making the queue opaque?
questions.whyItMatters: The current heuristic is inspectable and deterministic, but short attention windows may repeatedly hide lower-ranked work.

areas.name: GitHub identity boundary
areas.purpose: Handles sign-in, read-only GitHub access, encrypted sessions, and the repository inventory.
areas.verdict: Access is limited by the GitHub App. Unattended token refresh is still unresolved.

walkthrough.title: Follow a private repository request
walkthrough.outcome: See where identity becomes repository access—and where the browser is deliberately prevented from receiving the GitHub token.

steps.label: Start GitHub authorization
steps.explanation: Baxtori creates a signed OAuth state value and redirects the browser to the private GitHub App.
steps.invariant: A callback is accepted only when it belongs to the authorization flow Baxtori started.

steps.label: Exchange and seal the session
steps.explanation: The callback verifies state, exchanges the temporary code, fetches the GitHub identity, and stores the result in an encrypted HttpOnly cookie.
steps.invariant: The access token stays server-side and is never returned to browser JavaScript.

steps.label: Recover server identity
steps.explanation: Authenticated route handlers decrypt the cookie, validate its lifetime, and refresh GitHub access when required.
steps.invariant: Every repository request begins from a valid, unexpired server session.

steps.label: Ask GitHub for the allowed set
steps.explanation: The server uses the recovered credential to list repositories visible to the signed-in user and the app installation.
steps.invariant: Private repositories appear only when access was explicitly granted through GitHub.

areas.name: Weekly rundown engine
areas.purpose: Collects changed commits from configured branches and records which candidates were published, deferred, excluded, quiet, or inaccessible.
areas.verdict: Collection is deterministic. The scheduled review still decides what becomes a story.

walkthrough.title: Follow authorized scope into the edition ledger
walkthrough.outcome: See how repository access, inspectable caches, and attention policy become explicit publication decisions.

steps.label: Export one account's intent and inventory
steps.explanation: The exporter requests account-scoped topics, questions, repository choices, and the authorized repository inventory into ignored scratch input.
steps.invariant: Compiler intent belongs to the configured GitHub account and never replaces the cache manifest.

steps.label: Classify requested repositories
steps.explanation: Configured sources, authorization metadata, reader modes, and selections become collected, metadata-only, missing, or muted decisions.
steps.invariant: Only a configured origin-backed cache can supply code evidence.

steps.label: Collect exact origin history
steps.explanation: The collector fetches the configured branch and indexes commits, file statistics, map impact, and targeted review requests.
steps.invariant: Local, staged, detached, and unpushed work cannot enter the evidence index.

steps.label: Pack and record publication choices
steps.explanation: Qualifying findings are priority ordered and fitted to a reading budget, with deferred and excluded work retained as decisions.
steps.invariant: Every published story and every quiet or inaccessible repository remains accounted for.

areas.name: Edition and evidence model
areas.purpose: Stores each story with its topic, repository, comparison commits, line ranges, verification steps, tradeoff, and publication record.
areas.verdict: Stories are versioned and must name a valid comparison and line range.

areas.name: Human reading loop
areas.purpose: Displays current and archived editions and stores watches, code questions, map states, and review requests.
areas.verdict: Reader state survives new editions. Queue ordering still uses fixed rules.

areas.name: Repository map engine
areas.purpose: Matches changed files to repository map areas and builds a time-limited queue from unfinished areas and questions.
areas.verdict: File matches can request another map review but cannot rewrite the map on their own.

walkthrough.title: Follow a diff into the study queue
walkthrough.outcome: See how Baxtori uses Git as a review trigger without pretending a changed filename explains what the code means.

steps.label: Collect exact repository evidence
steps.explanation: The deterministic prepass records commits and touched files from the configured review window.
steps.invariant: The collector describes observable Git history and does not invent an explanation.

steps.label: Match changes to mapped evidence
steps.explanation: Changed paths are matched against each area's evidence after the map's review cursor.
steps.invariant: A match can reopen review, but cannot change confidence or meaning by itself.

steps.label: Record reviewed knowledge
steps.explanation: The judgment-heavy review updates areas, questions, and an append-only review event with an exact through-commit.
steps.invariant: Every durable map claim retains inspectable evidence and an explicit uncertainty boundary.

steps.label: Fit the next lesson to the reader
steps.explanation: The interface ranks unfinished areas and open questions, then packs them into the reader's chosen time budget.
steps.invariant: The queue stays small enough to consume and always allows the reader to skip or defer work.

areas.name: Account-backed review feedback loop
areas.purpose: Stores one account's watches, code questions, map states, repository modes, inventory, and review requests.
areas.verdict: Accounts are isolated. Feedback export still fails as a whole if repository inventory is unavailable.

walkthrough.title: Follow an evidence question into the next review
walkthrough.outcome: See how a reader preserves one exact uncertainty without broadening it to another story or repository.

steps.label: Validate the evidence address
steps.explanation: Repository, comparison commits, safe path, and a bounded line range are parsed before the question is accepted.
steps.invariant: A durable question always points to a specific inspectable code range.

steps.label: Persist the question under its topic
steps.explanation: Convex binds the question to its reader, topic thread, edition, story, lens, guidance, and exact evidence.
steps.invariant: Question evidence cannot cross into another repository.

steps.label: Export only actionable threads
steps.explanation: The compiler receives active watches and open queued questions, leaving snoozed, resolved, and merely historical state out.
steps.invariant: Only explicit current intent can influence the next review.

steps.label: Acknowledge inspected requests exactly
steps.explanation: Completion accepts only IDs present in the exported queue and marks those exact records processed.
steps.invariant: Failed, inaccessible, and unrelated requests remain queued.

areas.name: Hosting transition
areas.purpose: Builds and deploys the same application to Vercel and Sites.
areas.verdict: Both builds are deployed; Vercel serves the public domain.

areas.name: Agent context export
areas.purpose: Planned export of reviewed repository notes for use by coding agents.
